official_functions.role: Name the packet id assigned in Play clientbound state
Construct, write, and decode the block_destruction packet body

official_functions.function_or_member: FriendlyByteBuf.readVarInt(), FriendlyByteBuf.writeVarInt(int), FriendlyByteBuf.readBlockPos(), FriendlyByteBuf.writeBlockPos(BlockPos), FriendlyByteBuf.readUnsignedByte(), FriendlyByteBuf.writeByte(int)
official_functions.role: Define the breaker id, block position, and progress field body shape
Write and dispatch-decode the full official Play clientbound block_destruction frame

invariants: The official Play clientbound table assigns block_destruction immediately after block_changed_ack.
The official Play clientbound protocol codec dispatches the block_destruction id to ClientboundBlockDestructionPacket.
The official block_destruction body is breaker id as FriendlyByteBuf VarInt, block position as FriendlyByteBuf BlockPos, and progress as an unsigned byte.
The fixture uses the public ClientboundBlockDestructionPacket(int, BlockPos, int) constructor, so it does not require initialized Minecraft/game state.

The framed bytes, packet table id, body bytes, decoded packet class, decoded breaker id, decoded block position, decoded progress, Play clientbound packet table, and remaining decode byte count are generated by the official jar-backed answer artifact.
This contract does not prove block break animation semantics, entity existence, client world state, initialized game state, spawn readiness, world load, render readiness, or client-load completion.
test_topology_note: rust_test_target names the current case adapter only; the manifest owns the runnable test topology.
stop_boundary: Do not use this framed dispatch/decode contract to infer block break animation semantics, entity existence, client world state, initialized game state, spawn readiness, world load, render readiness, or client-load completion.
